Class CachedPacket

java.lang.Object
net.minestom.server.network.packet.server.CachedPacket
All Implemented Interfaces:
SendablePacket
@Internal public final class CachedPacket extends Object implements SendablePacket
Represents a packet whose framed representation is computed lazily and cached for reuse.

The cached value is stored in a SoftReference, allowing it to be reclaimed in response to memory pressure. The value is recomputed when the reference has been cleared or when invalidate() is called.

When caching is enabled, cache computation is serialized so that only one thread invokes the packet supplier and computes a replacement value at a time. Cache reads and publication use acquire and release memory semantics.

When caching is disabled, calls to packet(ConnectionState) invoke the packet supplier directly. The supplier must therefore be thread safe if this object may be accessed concurrently while caching is disabled.

The supplied packet and its serialized representation must remain valid for the ConnectionState used to populate the cache. A CachedPacket should not be shared across connection states when the resulting framed representation differs between those states.

  • Constructor Details

    • CachedPacket

      public CachedPacket(Supplier<ServerPacket> packetSupplier)
      Creates a cached packet backed by the given packet supplier.

      The supplier is invoked whenever the framed packet must be recomputed. It may therefore be invoked multiple times over the lifetime of this object.

      When caching is enabled, supplier invocation is serialized. When caching is disabled, the supplier may be invoked concurrently and must be thread safe if this object is accessed by multiple threads.

      Parameters:
      packetSupplier - the packet supplier
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if packetSupplier is null

    • CachedPacket

      public CachedPacket(ServerPacket packet)
      Creates a cached packet backed by a constant packet value.
      Parameters:
      packet - the packet to frame and cache
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if packet is null

  • Method Details

    • invalidate

      public void invalidate()
      Invalidates the currently cached packet, if any.

      The next cache access recomputes the packet unless caching is disabled. This method has no effect when caching is disabled.

      Implementation Requirements:
      This method clears the cached reference using release semantics. Cache computation is not synchronized with invalidation, so an in progress computation may publish a value after this method returns.

    • packet

      public ServerPacket packet(ConnectionState state)
      Returns the packet represented by this cached value.

      When caching is enabled, this method may initialize or reuse the cached framed packet. Supplier invocation and cache computation are serialized.

      When caching is disabled, the packet supplier is invoked directly and may be invoked concurrently by multiple callers.

      Parameters:
      state - the connection state used when framing the packet
      Returns:
      the packet value

    • body

      @Nullable public @Nullable NetworkBuffer body(ConnectionState state)
      Returns the cached framed packet body for the given connection state.

      If caching is enabled, this method initializes the cache when necessary. Cache computation and supplier invocation are serialized.

      If caching is disabled, no framed body is created and null is returned.

      Parameters:
      state - the connection state used when framing the packet
      Returns:
      the framed packet body, or null when caching is disabled

    • isValid

      public boolean isValid()
      Returns whether a framed packet is currently cached and has not been reclaimed.

      This result is inherently transient because the underlying soft reference may be cleared or the cache may be invalidated immediately after this method returns.

      Returns:
      true if caching is enabled and a cached value is currently available

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Returns a string representation containing the currently referenced cached value.

      Calling this method does not invoke the packet supplier or repopulate the cache.

      Overrides:
      toString in class Object
      Returns:
      a string representation of this cached packet
